summary: Provides Appwrite auth state and session actions to the Next.js app.
responsibilities: Load the current Appwrite account on app initialization.
Expose email/password login, Google OAuth login, registration, logout, ping, user refresh, billing status refresh, and Stripe checkout start actions.
Represent loading, user, ping status, and Appwrite errors in React state.

functions.name: AuthProvider
functions.summary: Wraps children with auth state and actions.

functions.name: refreshUser
functions.summary: Reads the current Appwrite account, refreshes billing status when authenticated, and clears user state when unauthenticated.

functions.name: refreshBillingStatus
functions.summary: Creates an Appwrite JWT for the current session and asks the server billing API for free or paid status.

functions.name: startCheckout
functions.summary: Creates an Appwrite JWT for the current session and asks the server checkout API for a Stripe Checkout URL.

functions.name: pingAppwrite
functions.summary: Checks Appwrite reachability and records success or error state.

functions.name: login
functions.summary: Creates an email/password session, then refreshes the current user.

functions.name: loginWithGoogle
functions.summary: Starts Appwrite Google OAuth with dashboard success and signin failure redirects.

functions.name: register
functions.summary: Creates an Appwrite account and then logs in using email/password.

functions.name: logout
functions.summary: Deletes the current session and clears local user state.

functions.name: useAuth
functions.summary: Requires consumers to be rendered within AuthProvider.

invariants: Authentication failures during refresh must leave user as null and clear billing status.
Billing status must come from /api/billing/status using an Appwrite JWT, not from trusted client-only state.
Stripe checkout must start through /api/billing/checkout using an Appwrite JWT, not from client-side Stripe secrets or client-provided price IDs.
Logout must clear the local user after deleting the current session.
OAuth redirects must remain same-origin paths unless policy is updated.
useAuth must fail loudly when used outside AuthProvider.
